---
title: "Notes — Fourier Transform"
date: 2026-03-26
categories: [math, signal-processing]
---

## Definition

The continuous Fourier transform of a function $f(t)$ is:

$$
\hat{f}(\xi) = \int_{-\infty}^{\infty} f(t)\, e^{-2\pi i \xi t}\, dt
$$

And the inverse:

$$
f(t) = \int_{-\infty}^{\infty} \hat{f}(\xi)\, e^{2\pi i \xi t}\, d\xi
$$

---

## Key properties

| Property | Time domain | Frequency domain |
|----------|-------------|------------------|
| Linearity | $\alpha f + \beta g$ | $\alpha \hat{f} + \beta \hat{g}$ |
| Time shift | $f(t - t_0)$ | $e^{-2\pi i \xi t_0} \hat{f}(\xi)$ |
| Convolution | $(f * g)(t)$ | $\hat{f}(\xi) \cdot \hat{g}(\xi)$ |
| Parseval | $\int |f|^2\, dt$ | $\int |\hat{f}|^2\, d\xi$ |

---

## Visualisation

The animation below (rendered in Manim) shows how a square wave decomposes
into a sum of sinusoids as more Fourier terms are added:

![Fourier series decomposition](images/fourier.gif){fig-align="center" width="80%"}

::: {.callout-note}
Replace `images/fourier.gif` with your own Manim output. Any `.gif` or `.mp4`
in the `images/` folder works — no extra config needed.
:::

---

## Worked example — Gaussian

For $f(t) = e^{-\pi t^2}$, the Fourier transform is its own Gaussian:

$$
\hat{f}(\xi) = e^{-\pi \xi^2}
$$

This is the only function (up to scaling) that is an eigenfunction of the
Fourier transform with eigenvalue 1.

If we want it to have normalization one, we then need to define:
$$
\hat{f}(\xi) = \frac{1}{\sqrt{2 \pi \sigma^2}} e^{-\pi \xi^2}
$$
---

## Interactive TNB Frame Animation

Enter a parametric curve $\vec{r}(t) = (x(t),\, y(t),\, z(t))$ as three
comma-separated expressions in $t$, then click **Render**.
Rendering takes roughly 60–120 seconds.

::: {=html}
<style>
  #tnb-form      { display:flex; gap:.5rem; flex-wrap:wrap; align-items:center; margin-bottom:1rem; }
  #curve-input   { flex:1 1 320px; font-family:monospace; font-size:.95rem;
                   padding:.4rem .6rem; border:1px solid #ccc; border-radius:4px; }
  #render-btn    { padding:.4rem 1.1rem; background:#0d6efd; color:#fff;
                   border:none; border-radius:4px; cursor:pointer; font-size:.95rem; }
  #render-btn:disabled { opacity:.55; cursor:not-allowed; }
  #tnb-spinner   { display:none; align-items:center; gap:.6rem; color:#555; margin-bottom:.75rem; }
  #tnb-spinner svg { animation:spin 1s linear infinite; }
  @keyframes spin  { to { transform:rotate(360deg); } }
  #tnb-error     { display:none; color:#c0392b; background:#fdf2f2;
                   border:1px solid #e8b4b4; border-radius:4px; padding:.6rem .9rem;
                   margin-bottom:.75rem; white-space:pre-wrap; font-size:.88rem; }
  #tnb-video     { display:none; width:100%; max-width:760px;
                   border-radius:6px; box-shadow:0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,.15); }
</style>

<div id="tnb-form">
  <input id="curve-input" type="text"
         value="2*cos(3*t), 2*sin(t), 2*cos(2*t)"
         placeholder="x(t), y(t), z(t)" />
  <button id="render-btn" onclick="renderTNB()">Render</button>
</div>

<div id="tnb-spinner">
  <svg width="20" height="20" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none"
       stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2.5">
    <path d="M12 2v4M12 18v4M4.93 4.93l2.83 2.83M16.24 16.24l2.83 2.83
             M2 12h4M18 12h4M4.93 19.07l2.83-2.83M16.24 7.76l2.83-2.83"/>
  </svg>
  <span>Rendering… this may take 60–120 seconds.</span>
</div>

<div id="tnb-error"></div>
<video id="tnb-video" controls playsinline></video>

<script>
const API_URL = "https://my-site-3737.onrender.com"; // update after deploy

function splitComponents(raw) {
  const parts = [];
  let depth = 0, cur = "";
  for (const ch of raw) {
    if      (ch === "(")             { depth++; cur += ch; }
    else if (ch === ")")             { depth--; cur += ch; }
    else if (ch === "," && depth === 0) { parts.push(cur.trim()); cur = ""; }
    else                             { cur += ch; }
  }
  if (cur.trim()) parts.push(cur.trim());
  return parts;
}

async function renderTNB() {
  const btn     = document.getElementById("render-btn");
  const spinner = document.getElementById("tnb-spinner");
  const errDiv  = document.getElementById("tnb-error");
  const video   = document.getElementById("tnb-video");
  const raw     = document.getElementById("curve-input").value.trim();

  errDiv.style.display  = "none";
  errDiv.textContent    = "";
  video.style.display   = "none";
  video.src             = "";
  btn.disabled          = true;
  spinner.style.display = "flex";

  try {
    const components = splitComponents(raw);
    if (components.length !== 3)
      throw new Error(`Expected 3 comma-separated components, got ${components.length}.`);

    const resp = await fetch(API_URL, {
      method:  "POST",
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
      body:    JSON.stringify({ components }),
    });

    if (!resp.ok) {
      let msg = `Server error ${resp.status}`;
      try { const j = await resp.json(); msg = j.detail ?? msg; } catch (_) {}
      throw new Error(msg);
    }

    const blob = await resp.blob();
    video.src           = URL.createObjectURL(blob);
    video.style.display = "block";
  } catch (err) {
    errDiv.textContent  = String(err.message ?? err);
    errDiv.style.display = "block";
  } finally {
    btn.disabled          = false;
    spinner.style.display = "none";
  }
}
</script>
:::

---

## Code block (optional)

You can also include executable Python code cells if you have Jupyter installed:

```{python}
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

t = np.linspace(-3, 3, 1000)
f = np.exp(-np.pi * t**2)
xi = np.fft.fftfreq(len(t), d=t[1]-t[0])
fhat = np.fft.fft(f) * (t[1]-t[0])

plt.plot(np.fft.fftshift(xi), np.abs(np.fft.fftshift(fhat)))
plt.xlabel(r"$\xi$")
plt.title("Magnitude spectrum")
plt.show()
```
